# FOIL Vacuum & AOT Parity Protocol

This file is the falsifiability ledger for the FOIL vacuum and the
certified numeric-loop AOT lane. It is the **contract ledger**, not a
results document: every claim here names the artifact that enforces it
and the artifact that would falsify it. Tone and structure follow
`../distributed-inference/ACKERMANN_CERTIFICATION_PROTOCOL.md`.

Sibling deliverables:

- `src/ackermann_admission.rs` — admission gate, `vacuum_licensed`,
  `gate_step_envelope`, `SessionVacuumLicense`.
- `src/bin/gnexec.rs` — the structural estimator
  (`estimate_step_envelope`), the vacuum wire-in, `--resident-walk`,
  and the walker the AOT lane must agree with.
- `src/aot_c.rs` — `numeric_loop_c`, the certified-class C emitter,
  and the checked-in parity harness (`numeric_loop_*` tests).
- `Gnosis/FoilVacuumAdmission.lean` (open-source/gnosis-math) — the
  vacuum theorems: guard vacuity, estimator soundness, sequential
  composition, session/stream licenses.
- `Gnosis/AotParityOracle.lean` (open-source/gnosis-math) — the parity
  pentads: pinned corpus values, the memory rule (`cGet_agrees`), the
  pure-index RMW license, and the beyond-hop-cap oracles.
- `Gnosis/Protocol69Wire.lean` (open-source/gnosis-math) +
  `distributed-inference/src/protocol69.rs` — the 32-byte wire
  envelope codec and its round-trip model: little-endian field
  encoding invertible on each field's domain (`le_roundtrip`,
  `envelope_roundtrip`), chunk widths exact (`leBytes_length`),
  widths summing to the frame with offsets as running sums; the Rust
  decoder refuses wrong lengths, wrong magic, and projection
  inconsistency (`wire_envelope_refuses_misreads`).

## 1. The vacuum contract

The FOIL admission gate certifies a program's growth class from its GG
topology (`loop_nesting` from unbounded-loop nesting depth,
`back_edges` for unstructured cycles, `call_cycles` for recursion). An
`AdmitPrimitiveRecursive` verdict licenses the vacuum: dropping the
wall-clock deadline guard, whose condition provably cannot fire on the
calibration window (`guard_vacuous_on_window`,
`vacuum_removal_is_noop_on_window`).

Honesty boundaries, each load-bearing:

- The `max_call_depth` OS-stack backstop and the per-topology hop cap
  are machine limits, not growth-class claims. They stay in force
  inside the vacuum.
- The vacuum auto-engages ONLY for structurally terminating programs
  (no unbounded loops, no unstructured cycles, no recursion —
  `structurally_terminating`). Loop-free tree recursion (naive fib) is
  the deadline's exact target and never auto-enters; certified loopy
  programs are opt-in (`--foil-vacuum` / `GNEXEC_FOIL_VACUUM=1`;
  `GNEXEC_FOIL_VACUUM=0` kills both modes).
- The license is a pure function of the topology
  (`request_license_constant`), so `--resident-walk` certifies once at
  load and never re-negotiates per request (`session_vacuum_noop`,
  `stream_vacuum_noop`) — sound exactly while the topology is
  immutable, i.e. the lifetime of the resident process.

Falsifier: any execution in which the vacuum changes an observable
result, or in which a non-certified shape engages it.

## 2. Measured reality (recorded, not promised)

- The deadline guard with pulse batching costs ~10–15µs per ~10k-hop
  walk — ~0.2% of walk time. The vacuum's wall-clock dividend is
  honest but below run noise everywhere measured (one-shot and
  resident). Its value is theorem-grade justification plus the
  multi_file completeness lift (certified deep-but-thin import chains
  resolve past the depth truncation).
- The walker's real costs were elsewhere: AST re-derivation per loop
  iteration (fixed: walk-local memos, 3.5×) and evaluator bookkeeping
  (fixed: Fx hashing, field-id pre-resolution, zero-alloc hot arms,
  another ~1.6×). Resident throughput: 189 → ~1035 req/s across those
  two rounds, byte-identical outputs.
- The remaining walker floor is AST-walking interpretation itself; the
  step past it is the AOT lane below.

## 3. The parity contract (AOT lane)

`numeric_loop_c` compiles the certified numeric-loop class to
standalone C. The lane's single invariant: **it never answers where
its answer could diverge from the walker.**

- It prints only integral f64 results in the 2^53-safe range,
  formatted exactly as the walker formats integers.
- Everything else **declines** with exit code 3 and the run falls back
  to the guarded walker: non-integral results, unparseable or non-flag
  inputs for 1-param entries, loop-fuel exhaustion (2^42 iterations,
  the compiled analogue of the deadline), implicit-undefined returns
  in helpers, and every memory violation (out-of-bounds, negative,
  non-integral, or NaN indexes — `arr_get`/`arr_set`).
- Outside-class constructs refuse to compile (`None`): recursion
  (self or mutual — the walker and admission gate own that class),
  arity mismatches, arrays in scalar positions or as call arguments,
  `Math.pow`/`Math.log*` (last-ulp drift between `powf` and `pow`),
  `Math.random` (nondeterministic), BigInt, strings, objects. The
  bitwise family is fully in-class on its provable domains: `& | ^`
  on integral |x| < 2^32 (the walker's `as i64` and C's cast agree),
  `<<` declining results that are not exactly f64-representable, `>>`
  declining negative operands (C's arithmetic shift of negatives is
  implementation-defined), `>>>` declining negative operands and
  counts (the walker's u32 saturation has no defined C mirror).
  Out-of-domain operands decline.
- Capability parity rule: a surface form enters the emitter only if
  the walker executes it too (augmented subscripts landed in both
  lanes in one commit). The AOT lane may exceed the walker only on
  SCALE (beyond the hop cap), never on shape.

Falsifiers: any input on which both lanes answer differently; any
decline case that answers; any outside-class shape that compiles.
Enforced by `aot_c::tests::numeric_loop_*` (compiled-and-executed when
`cc` is present) and the Lean pentads below.

## 4. The oracle pentads

`Gnosis/AotParityOracle.lean` pins the corpus values both lanes must
produce — and, for the beyond-hop-cap cases, the values only the
compiled lane CAN produce, where Lean is the second witness because
differential testing has no interpreter output to diff against:

| # | Program shape                  | Pinned value(s)            |
|---|--------------------------------|----------------------------|
| 1 | literal-trip loop              | 45                         |
| 2 | sum loop @ 900                 | 404550                     |
| 3 | sum loop @ 2,000,000           | 1999999000000 (Lean-only)  |
| 4 | four sequential loops @ 5      | 50                         |
| 5 | decline boundary (n/4)         | 2/4 declines, 8/4 = 2      |
| 6 | helper composition @ 10 / 10⁶  | 90 / 999999000000          |
| 7 | exact Math intrinsics @ 9      | 16                         |
| 8 | histogram @ 10 / 10⁶           | 30 / 3000000               |
| 9 | augmented-subscript histogram  | = #8 (same function)       |
| 10| convolution kernel             | 161                        |
| 11| protocol69 projection (66 ^ 7) | 69, roundtrip recovers 66  |
| 12| complete bitwise family        | 361; 3<<31 answers exact   |

Structural obligations alongside the values: `cGet_agrees` (the
memory rule — bounds-checked access equals the walker's Option-typed
lookup everywhere), `pure_index_double_eval` (the RMW license),
`seq_composition_certified` vs `nesting_can_breach` (why sequential
loops share a degree and nested ones escalate), and
`estimator_cubic_breaches_window` (why degree ≥ 3 never certifies).

## 5. How to extend the class

Harness-first, one construct per change:

1. Demote the construct's refusal test to a parity test.
2. If its semantics can diverge (formatting, memory, precision), add a
   decline rule before adding the answer path.
3. If the walker doesn't execute it, land walker support in the SAME
   change (capability parity).
4. Pin one canonical program in the Lean pentad; prefer a closed form
   over a million-step reduction for large oracles.
5. `cargo test --lib aot_c` and `lake build Gnosis.AotParityOracle`
   must both be green before push.
